SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.storage

Class NPointer

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNField
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNElementary
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNNumeric
com.nib.commons.storage.NInteger
com.nib.commons.storage.NPointer
All Implemented Interfaces:
IAddress, IDataArea, IntegerVariable, Cloneable
public class NPointer extends NInteger implements IAddress
Persistent pointer to a memory location.

The pointer stores both offset and memory of the referred area in the allocate memory - The memory is stored as an index in the first byte of the area - The offset is stored in the 2nd, trd and 4th bytes of the pointer area.

The memory index refers to an item in a static list. One list per thread exists, and it gets reset when the context is initialized.

So if the memory segment where the pointer is allocated is copied or addressed to another segment the pointer is still valid, as long as staying in the same thread.

For example: - pointer-1 is allocated in the memory segment-1. It addresses area-x in segment-x - segment-1 contains the offset part of the pointer-1 - area-lk is not allocated. its address is set to pointer-1. - if area-lk is now accessed from the same thread, it works. If accessed from a different thread, it leads to unpredictable results.
